file_name: Cerus Corporation - FIRST AMEND TO SUPPLY AND MANUFACTURING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: In no event shall any Purchase Order: require delivery of more than [*] units of [*] and [*] units of [*] in any [*] (the "Porex Capacity Limitations").
perturbation.changed_text: In no event shall any Purchase Order: require delivery of more than [*] units of [*] and [*] units of [*] in any [*]. All claims are subject to arbitration (the "Porex Capacity Limitations").
perturbation.explanation: By inserting an arbitration clause at the end of a sentence that defines capacity limitations, this creates ambiguity and makes the clause less enforceable, as it is not clearly labeled as a dispute resolution mechanism.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Federal Arbitration Act favors arbitration agreements, but for them to be valid, they must be clear and unambiguous. Placing an arbitration clause in an odd location raises doubts about its intentionality and scope, thus reducing its enforceability.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate U.S. Code Notes prev | next A written provision in any maritime transaction or a contract evidencing a transaction involving commerce to settle by arbitration a controversy thereafter arising out of such contract or transaction, or the refusal to perform the whole or any part thereof, or an agreement in writing to submit to arbitration an existing controversy arising out of such a contract, transaction, or refusal, shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract or as otherwise provided in chapter 4. (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 670 ; Pub. L. 117–90, § 2(b)(1)(A) , Mar. 3, 2022 , 136 Stat. 27 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 2, 43 Stat. 883 . Editorial Notes Amendments 2022— Pub. perturbation.justification: The altered contract includes an arbitration clause within the section defining "Porex Capacity Limitations," which is a structural flaw. The Perturbation Explanation states this creates ambiguity and reduces the enforceability of the arbitration clause. The Law Explanation says that arbitration agreements must be clear and unambiguous to be valid. Both Scraped Law Snippet 1 and Scraped Law Snippet 2, referencing 9 USC 2, state that agreements to arbitrate must be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable. By placing the arbitration clause in an unclear location, the modification undermines the clarity required for a valid arbitration agreement, which contradicts the legal requirement for enforceability as stated in 9 USC 2. This makes the contract non-compliant and creates a legal contradiction.
